opinion.h4: VAUGHN v. RODRIGUEZ, ACTING WARDEN. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE TENTH CIRCUIT. No. 1149, Misc. Decided June 3, 1968.
opinion.p: Boston E. Witt, Attorney General of New Mexico, and Thomas O. Olson, Special Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the United States Court of Appeals for the Tenth Circuit for further consideration in light of Peyton v. Rowe, ante, p. 54.
391 U.S. 603 PEART v. CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 1445, Misc. Decided June 3, 1968.
67 Cal. 2d 866, 434 P.2d 609, appeal dismissed and certiorari denied.
The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
